Now, considering geographic diversity. The network topology has mostly US and Germany. So validators with domains from underrepresented countries (like Sweden, Bulgaria, Canada, France, Finland) would get a boost. But in the validator list, most domains are .org or .com, not sure of their actual location. Only a few have verified domains. For example, the one with app.w.ai (verified) is in the US maybe? But the country distribution shows US has 24, so even if they are in US, no boost. The validator with domain in Sweden (postfiat.nlh.xyz) might be in Sweden, which has 1 node. So that could add a diversity bonus. Similarly, Bulgaria has one node, but the validator with domain in Bulgaria? Not sure. The validator with domain postfiat.nlh.xyz has country Sweden, so that's underrepresented. So their score could be boosted a bit.

So adjusting for diversity: validators with domains in countries with low representation (Sweden, Bulgaria, Canada, France) get a slight boost. But since most domains are not verified, the boost might be minimal. For example, the validator with domain in Sweden (postfiat.nlh.xyz) would get a diversity boost. Similarly, preaware.org (domain verified) but not sure where that is. Maybe in US? If not, then maybe another country.

Also, looking at the server versions: all are on 3.0.0, so no issues there. All are up to date. Fee votes are all 10, so aligned.

So, putting it all together. For each validator, base score is based on consensus, then operational (domain verified), then diversity.

Validator nHUgi9MxYymj7CGS48gjm9TPZQcfRA47XUHFk9Vu5wDq1PqTZdYr: domain verified (app.w.ai), but 0.936 over 30 days. Lower score, maybe 75-80.
